metadata.title: hexa-bio v2 — 5-axis core + 4-axis expansion-layer record + 15 sub-axes + cross-axis bridges + disease-portfolio case studies (in-silico simulator-consistency)
metadata._title_alt_short: hexa-bio: HEXA-Bio Molecular Toolkit — expansion-layer snapshot, 2026-05-16
metadata._title_user_action: Choose final form (long vs short); see USER_INPUT_CHECKLIST.md item 3.
metadata.description: Repository snapshot of the hexa-bio HEXA-Bio Molecular Toolkit at session 2026-05-16. The 5-axis core (QUANTUM, WEAVE, NANOBOT, RIBOZYME, VIROCAPSID) is UNCHANGED from v1. This snapshot adds a USER-DIRECTED expansion-layer record (AXIS/HIERARCHY.tape) registering 4 expansion-main axes (COVALENT, BIFUNCTIONAL, METALLODRUG, OLIGONUCLEOTIDE) and 15 sub-axes (PROTAC, LYTAC, AUTAC, RIBOTAC, COVALENT-DEGRADER, MOLECULAR-GLUE under BIFUNCTIONAL; ALLOSTERIC, CRYPTIC-POCKET, PPI under QUANTUM; PEPTIDE, MACROCYCLE under WEAVE; REVERSIBLE-COVALENT under COVALENT; APTAMER, RNA-TARGETING-SMALL-MOLECULE under RIBOZYME; CAPSID-ASSEMBLY-MODULATOR under VIROCAPSID), plus cross-axis bridges, disease-portfolio case studies, and a layer of `selftest/*_gate.{py,sh}` infrastructure scripts. The rigorous 'keep-5' axis-expansion dissent (AXIS/README.md §4) is preserved verbatim — NOT erased. All C2/C3 PASS verdicts verify simulator + metadata internal consistency ONLY. NO therapeutic, clinical, regulatory, immunogenic, efficacy, potency, selectivity, or wet-lab claim is made or implied (AGENTS.tape g8/f2). External drug-modality axes are described by their OWN published precedents (Strelow 2017 covalent kinetics; Douglass 2013/Han 2020 ternary-complex cooperativity; Griffith-Orgel CFSE; SantaLucia 1998 NN), NOT lattice-fit (g3/f1). The n=6 invariant lattice is a TOOL, never a derivation rule (g2). All external-contact items (wet-lab CRO outreach, IP counsel, FDA pre-IND, ORCID/byline decisions, and the Zenodo deposit itself) remain user-owned per g9 external-contact-defer; see USER_ACTION_REQUIRED.md.

creators._USER_ACTION_REQUIRED: Fill name, ORCID, affiliation, email per USER_INPUT_CHECKLIST.md items 1-2. Agent does NOT pre-fill external-identity surfaces.
creators.name: PLACEHOLDER_LAST_FIRST

metadata._keywords_user_action: Trim to ~10 per Zenodo guideline; see USER_INPUT_CHECKLIST.md item 4.
metadata._license_alt: CC-BY-4.0 (Zenodo academic default; appropriate if paper-text-only)
metadata._license_user_action: Choose Apache-2.0 vs CC-BY-4.0; see USER_INPUT_CHECKLIST.md item 5.

related_identifiers._USER_ACTION_REQUIRED: Iff v1 was actually deposited, fill its DOI here; see USER_INPUT_CHECKLIST.md item 6. Agent has no on-file evidence v1 was deposited.

metadata._communities_note: None at draft time — user-controlled curation.
metadata._version_note: Working tag; user may choose semver (e.g. 2.0.0-draft) at deposit time.

_files_note: Final tarball composition is user-decided. Excludes wetlab/ USER_ACTION_REQUIRED items and any _absorption_bridge/ adapter with NON-COMMERCIAL RESEARCH ONLY licence flags pending re-review.
_deposit_command_REFERENCE_ONLY_DO_NOT_R: User-owned step. Token MUST live in $ZENODO_TOKEN env. NEVER embed token in metadata.json. NEVER commit token to git. Agent will not execute this command (g9).
_token_policy: ZENODO_TOKEN belongs in environment variable only. This JSON file MUST NOT contain a token, secret, API key, or any auth surface. If a future tool reads this file, it must refuse to proceed unless $ZENODO_TOKEN is set in the environment at runtime.
